message:
refactor: clippy and idiomatic Rust improvements across tests, benches, and conformance

Systematic cleanup addressing clippy lints and Rust idiom improvements:

Slice-from-ref optimization (avoid needless clone):
- macaroon.rs: &[bound.clone()] -> std::slice::from_ref(&bound)
- traceability.rs: &[file.clone()] -> std::slice::from_ref(&file)

map/unwrap_or -> map_or:
- demo_record_nondeterministic.rs: .map(|m| m.len()).unwrap_or(0) -> .map_or(0, ...)
- property_region_ops.rs: .map(|entries| ...).unwrap_or(0) -> .map_or(0, ...)

is_ok_and:
- multi_protocol_e2e.rs: .map(|r| r.status == ...).unwrap_or(false) -> .is_ok_and(...)

Unused result suppression:
- cancel_trace_bench.rs: black_box(result) -> black_box(result.is_ok())

Magic number -> hex literal:
- protocol_benchmark.rs: 126/127 -> 0x7E/0x7F (WebSocket length markers)
- net_http_ws_invariants.rs: 61/62 -> 0x3D/0x3E (HPACK static table indices)

Duration::from_mins preference:
- supervision.rs: from_secs(120) -> from_mins(2), from_secs(300) -> from_mins(5)
- pool.rs: from_secs(300) -> from_mins(5), from_secs(1800) -> from_mins(30)

Checked arithmetic:
- allocation_audit.rs: allocs * 1000 / ops -> checked_mul/checked_div chain

Lock scope tightening:
- task_handle.rs: Extract values before assertions to avoid holding RwLock
- replay_e2e_suite.rs: Tighten lock guard lifetime

Redundant wrapping removal:
- runner.rs: Box::pin(async move { future.await }) -> Box::pin(future)
- runner.rs: Remove unused RawWaker/RawWakerVTable/Waker imports
- golden_probe.rs: Inline temporary, remove redundant & on params
- property_cancellation.rs: Move .is_ok() into masked closure

Misc:
- runtime_state_refinement_map.rs: Add allow(unused_imports) for test cfg

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
